title: bistorta suffulta subsp. pergracilis
intro: Bistorta suffulta subsp. pergracilis, also known as small bistort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin issues. The health benefits of Bistorta suffulta subsp. pergracilis include its ability to reduce swelling, promote tissue repair, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns, such as digestive issues and skin conditions.
